name: golang-github-mitchellh-mapstructure-dev
title: golang-github-mitchellh-mapstructure-dev
summary: library for decoding generic map values into native Go structures
description:
library for decoding generic map values into native Go structures
mapstructure is a Go library for decoding generic map values to structures and
vice versa, while providing helpful error handling.
.
This library is most useful when decoding values from some data stream (JSON,
Gob, etc.) where you don't quite know the structure of the underlying data
until you read a part of it. You can therefore read a map[string]interface{}
and use this library to decode it into the proper underlying native Go
structure.
.
Go offers fantastic standard libraries for decoding formats such as JSON. The
standard method is to have a struct pre-created, and populate that struct from
the bytes of the encoded format. This is great, but the problem is if you have
configuration or an encoding that changes slightly depending on specific
fields.
.
Perhaps we can't populate a specific structure without first reading the "type"
field from the JSON. We could always do two passes over the decoding of the
JSON (reading the "type" first, and the rest later). However, it is much
simpler to just decode this into a map[string]interface{} structure, read the
"type" key, then use something like this library to decode it into the proper
structure.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use golang-github-mitchellh-mapstructure-dev because library for decoding generic map values into native Go structures.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use golang-github-mitchellh-mapstructure-dev as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair golang-github-mitchellh-mapstructure-dev with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `golang-github-mitchellh-mapstructure-dev --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
